map_description: Standard exploratory stellar neighbourhood — 56 systems charted

systems.name: Ondari Prime
systems.description: The Imperial Capital system. Three pristine continental worlds form the political and economic core of the empire.

systems.name: Solaria
systems.description: Early interstellar colony. Highly dense agricultural world and a secondary ocean world with extensive floating cities.

systems.name: Vandor Alpha
systems.description: Massive automated mining rigs stripping a dense asteroid belt rich in heavy metals.

systems.name: Zephyrus
systems.description: Twin terrestrial planets with dense nitrogen-oxygen atmospheres; major cultural and artistic centers.

systems.name: Caelum Minor
systems.description: A chilly but breathable world dedicated to terraforming research and massive seed-banking vaults.

systems.name: The Anvil
systems.description: Industrial shipyard hubs utilizing the intense gravitational field of a dead star core for precision hull forging.

systems.name: Xanadu
systems.description: Tropical paradise worlds acting as the primary luxury tourism and medical convalescence sectors for the empire.

systems.name: Boreas
systems.description: An ice-locked world with vast subterranean geothermal cities and heavy lithium mining operations.

systems.name: Helios Flare
systems.description: A hyper-active flare star studied for its unique magneto-hydrodynamic disruptions to sub-light communications.

systems.name: Thalassa
systems.description: A triple-planet system consisting of two sprawling archipelago worlds and a dry savanna moon.

systems.name: Krypton's Edge
systems.description: Heavy gravity mining outpost harvesting super-dense silicate crystals from a fractured rocky planetesimal.

systems.name: New Avalon
systems.description: Major breadbasket system with two terraformed planets supplying agricultural goods to the inner core.

systems.name: Obsidian
systems.description: Volcanically active moons harvested for rare rare-earth elements and high-grade industrial diamonds.

systems.name: Geryon
systems.description: A dry desert world famous for its deep canyon biomes and ancient fossilized silicon-based structures.

systems.name: Hyperion Gas
systems.description: Atmospheric skimming platforms extracting helium-3 and deuterium from two colossal gas giants.

systems.name: Tantalus
systems.description: Rich in mineral deposits and arable land; hosts a fiercely independent network of mining metropolises.

systems.name: The Chronos Void
systems.description: A rapidly spinning pulsar creating intense time-dilation boundary fields used to test experimental FTL shielding.

systems.name: Arcadia
systems.description: Stunning ringed habitable world with a densely populated temperate moon acting as a trading gateway.

systems.name: Ragnarok
systems.description: Iron-core planet stripped down by orbital kinetic pulverizers for bulk structural steel manufacturing.

systems.name: Castalia
systems.description: A vast ocean planet hosting thousands of floating aquacultural platforms and bio-pharmaceutical labs.

systems.name: Elysium
systems.description: Three highly urbanized ecumenopolis worlds acting as the secondary administrative nexus of the outer rim.

systems.name: Styx Deep
systems.description: Dark sector outpost extracting dark matter remnants and exotic matter from an ancient accretion disk.

systems.name: Valhalla
systems.description: Rugged mountain worlds settled by industrial clans; major manufacturing centers for atmospheric scrubbers.

systems.name: Phlegethon
systems.description: Super-heated mining outposts built inside hollowed-out asteroids orbiting perilously close to the star.

systems.name: Olympius
systems.description: High-gravity terrestrial worlds with sprawling subterranean military academies and defense bastions.

systems.name: Titan's Cradle
systems.description: Methane-sea moon outpost processing advanced hydrocarbon polymers and synthetic starship fuels.

systems.name: Lethe
systems.description: A foggy, marshy world with a highly complex native flora ecosystem heavily studied for genetic engineering.

systems.name: The Siren Stars
systems.description: A spectacular pair of bright blue-white stars emitting a harmonic radio resonance that scrambles navigation arrays.

systems.name: Asgard
systems.description: Twin alpine worlds characterized by massive evergreen biomes and high-tech civilian research enclaves.

systems.name: Vulkan Forge
systems.description: A massive complex of orbital foundries smelting heavy plating for the Imperial starfleet.

systems.name: Calypso
systems.description: A misty planet covered in vast fungal forests and bioluminescent biomes; major luxury pharmaceutical source.

systems.name: Tartarus Ring
systems.description: An extensively mined planetary debris ring containing ultra-rare radioactive isotopes.

systems.name: Hesperia
systems.description: Terraced agricultural worlds utilizing highly advanced orbital mirror arrays to maximize crop yields.

systems.name: Prometheus
systems.description: A geologically young world with frequent tectonic shifts, settled for its highly accessible surface minerals.

systems.name: The Maw
systems.description: A localized gravitational singularity surrounded by an empire-funded research station studying quantum foam mechanics.

systems.name: Avalon Minor
systems.description: Quiet, low-density colony worlds focused on academic research, historical archives, and cybernetics.

systems.name: Cimmeria
systems.description: Perpetually dark mining colony on a tidally locked world, harvesting deep-crust radioactive ores.

systems.name: Osiris
systems.description: A scorching desert planet with hab-domes built around deep underground aquifers; major silicon refinery hub.

systems.name: Nirvana
systems.description: A pristine triple-world paradise system carefully managed to prevent over-industrialization.

systems.name: Gargantua Orbital
systems.description: A colossal ring-station built around a gas giant to manage a fleet of 500+ automated resource harvesters.

systems.name: Nexus Core
systems.description: A highly unstable, violent giant star throwing off bizarre isotopic radiation fields at the very edge of imperial space.

systems.name: Gliese-881
systems.description: Unexplored system. Long-range telemetry indicates 0 habitable planets.

systems.name: Kepler Sector 4A
systems.description: Unexplored system. Spectroscopic analysis indicates 2 potentially habitable planets.

systems.name: HD-10934
systems.description: Unexplored system. Deep-space scans reveal 1 habitable candidate.

systems.name: Vela Minor
systems.description: Unexplored system. High radiation environment with 0 habitable planets.

systems.name: Outpost Omega-9
systems.description: Unexplored system. Automated probes detect 1 marginally habitable world.

systems.name: LHS-292
systems.description: Unexplored system. Stellar remnant surrounded by debris. 0 habitable planets.

systems.name: Eridani Traverse
systems.description: Unexplored system. Promising data implies 3 habitable candidate worlds.

systems.name: Wolf 1061 Edge
systems.description: Unexplored system. Initial scans show 1 terrestrial planet in the habitable zone.

systems.name: Centauri Reach
systems.description: Unexplored system. Tidally locked worlds detected; 0 currently classified as habitable.

systems.name: Trappist Terminus
systems.description: Unexplored system. Packed orbital resonance with 4 potentially habitable planets.

systems.name: Cygnus Void
systems.description: Unexplored system. High-energy young star. 0 habitable planets.

systems.name: Tauri-7
systems.description: Unexplored system. Long-range telemetry indicates 2 habitable planets.

systems.name: Sirius Deep
systems.description: Unexplored binary system. Massive astrometric wobble detected. 0 habitable planets.

systems.name: Altair Boundary
systems.description: Unexplored system. Atmospheric signatures suggest 1 highly habitable ocean world.

systems.name: Zenith-Prime
systems.description: Unexplored system. Sensor ghosts indicate 1 potential habitable planet in a highly eccentric orbit.
